data.name: Kpi_TotalReturn_Formula
data.value: Total Return (%) = (Market Value + Net Sales Proceeds + Net Dividends − Invested Capital) / Invested Capital × 100

data.name: Kpi_MarketValue_Formula
data.value: Market Value = Number of Shares × Current Price

data.name: Kpi_InvestedCapital_Formula
data.value: Invested Capital = Σ Purchase Amounts + Fees (FIFO cost basis of currently held shares)

data.name: Kpi_Cagr_Formula
data.value: CAGR = ((Market Value + Net Sales Proceeds + Net Dividends) / Invested Capital) ^ (1 / Years) − 1

data.name: Kpi_Irr_Formula
data.value: Present Value = Cash Flow / Discount Factor | Discount Factor = (1 + r)^t | IRR = r, such that Σ Present Values = 0

data.name: Kpi_TotalReturn_Description
data.value: Shows the percentage total return of the security. The breakdown below shows how the absolute components add up.

data.name: Kpi_MarketValue_Description
data.value: The current market value of the holding, calculated from the shares held and the most recently available price.

data.name: Kpi_InvestedCapital_Description
data.value: The total amount paid for the currently held shares, including all fees. Calculated using the FIFO method: sales reduce the oldest purchase lots first.

data.name: Kpi_Cagr_Description
data.value: Average annual growth rate assuming uniform growth. Years = period from the first purchase to today in full years.

data.name: Kpi_Irr_Description
data.value: The Internal Rate of Return (IRR) is the personal annualised return, weighted by the actual timing of each cash inflow and outflow. It answers: if I had invested my money at a fixed interest rate instead, which rate would have produced exactly the same final result? The earlier a purchase and the later a dividend or sale, the more the return is affected. The calculated rate is the value r at which the sum of all discounted cash flows (present values) equals zero. The cash flows with their present values are listed chronologically below.

data.name: Group_Irr_RateNote
data.value: r = {0} % – The rate at which the sum of all present values equals 0

data.name: Group_Irr_SignNote
data.value: Negative value = outflow (purchase), Positive value = inflow (dividend / market value)

data.name: Group_IrrProbe_OK
data.value: Equals 0 – the IRR rate is correctly calculated

data.name: Group_IrrProbe_Rounding
data.value: Rounding difference: The deviation of {0} EUR is caused by amounts rounded to 2 decimal places for display. The internal calculation yields exactly 0.

data.name: Item_TotalReturn_Note
data.value: = Market Value + Net Sales Proceeds − Invested Capital + Net Dividends
